name: Shrimp Creole
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 tbsp Fresh thyme or 2 tsp Dried thyme
1 tbsp Fresh basil or 2 tsp Dried basil
1 cup Green onions, chopped (green parts only)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp. Place shrimp heads (if available) and peels in a small saucepan with water. Bring to a slow boil over medium-high heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes. Strain and discard heads and peels.
2. Heat oil in a Dutch oven or large, heavy pot over medium-high heat. Add onions, peppers, and celery; sauté, stirring often, until vegetables are very soft (about 45 minutes).
3. Stir in tomatoes, salt, red and black pepper, thyme, basil, sugar, and shrimp stock. Return to a simmer.
4. Reduce heat to medium and let simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally. The creole sauce can be prepared up to 2 days in advance; refrigerate and it will improve after sitting overnight.
5. To serve, return sauce to a simmer and add shrimp. Cook until shrimp turn pink (5-7 minutes). Stir in green onions and parsley; cook for 1 minute more.
6. Serve over beds of rice on flat plates. Serves 6-8.
Shrimp and Ham or Tasso Jambalaya:
Follow steps 1-4 above to prepare the creole sauce, then add about a pound of cubed ham (or smoked turkey) and a 4-ounce can of tomato sauce to the finished sauce. Simmer an additional 45 minutes. Meanwhile, boil or steam 2-3 cups raw rice. Finish by adding shrimp, green onions, and parsley as in step 5. Use leftover creole sauce by reheating it with ham and tomato sauce before adding shrimp at the end of cooking. Serve over hot cooked rice.
